title: Review: daemon/relay/core round 27
notes:
Maintainer risk: HIGH — Three P0 correctness bugs (WHISPER no rate limit, GROUP_CREATE no rate limit, GROUP_SEND empty payload fan-out) are directly exploitable by any authenticated user without a subscription (WHISPER, GROUP_SEND) or with one (GROUP_CREATE). The insert_message_ext atomicity gap is a crash-window data integrity bug. The daemon has no HTTP rate limiting on any endpoint (existing nie-0j6b.7 + expanded scope in nie-nbqp.5). These are not theoretical — they all have plausible triggers reachable by a single malicious client.
Confidence: 20% chance of missed bug. Uncertainty driven by: (1) did not read daemon/jmap.rs handlers — they are the primary callers of daemon/store.rs and may have additional input validation gaps; (2) daemon/api.rs handle_wallet_pay holds wallet_pay_lock across balance_with_confirmations which is async DB I/O — the lock is an async Mutex so this is not executor-blocking, but the lock scope covers more than needed; (3) ws.rs has ~2500 lines and was read in chunks — a cross-handler interaction (e.g. race between GROUP_LEAVE and GROUP_SEND that removes a user after list_group_members but before enqueue) was noted but has a mitigation (broadcast_to_group re-checks membership). Would raise confidence if: jmap.rs handlers were reviewed, integration tests exercising concurrent GROUP operations existed.
